source: 1 m LiDAR terrain read · Penn State Deer-Forest rules

features.title: South-face beds
features.detail: SSE-facing bench near 1892 ft, ~29° — thermal sun and a downhill escape; Penn State collar deer bed exactly this.
features.windNote: Beds hold a NW wind. Approach from the ridge above. Thermals: at dawn scent drains SE (downhill); by midday it rises NW (upslope) — cross-check that against the wind.

features.title: Saddle crossing
features.detail: A ridge saddle near 1759 ft — deer cross ridges at their low gaps, funneling travel between bedding areas. Prime stand.
features.windNote: Sit the downhill side on a crosswind. Thermals: scent sinks downhill at dawn/dusk, rises upslope by midday — let the active thermal, not just the wind, pick your side.

features.title: Bench trail (1388 ft)
features.detail: A near-level shelf on a steep hillside — deer sidehill along benches like this at a constant elevation to save energy, traveling the lower slopes parallel to the ridge rather than climbing over the top.
features.windNote: Deer walk it into the wind; sit the downwind end, above or below the trail. Thermals: scent sinks downhill at dawn/dusk, rises upslope by midday — let the active thermal, not just the wind, pick your side.

features.title: Draw travel corridor
features.detail: A drainage carrying bed-to-feed travel and your quietest, lowest-scent way in.
features.windNote: Thermals: scent sinks downhill at dawn/dusk, rises upslope by midday — let the active thermal, not just the wind, pick your side. Hunt the lower end at dawn, the upper end midday.
